beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and quick breakfast at home while checking weather and work emails.
beats.reasoning: Layla is a morning person who starts early; the drizzle outside means she'll adjust her commute timing to avoid the SR-520 closure window.

beats.activity: Drive south to Renton via I-5, avoiding the SR-520 eastbound closure (9am–noon).
beats.reasoning: Leaving early ensures she clears the cross-lake traffic snarl; the SR-520 closure doesn't affect the I-5 route to Renton, but traffic will be heavy near the bridge.

beats.activity: Teach four periods of high school science labs, including one junior class titration session.
beats.reasoning: Standard teaching day; rain outside means indoor lab work is on schedule, no field trips planned.

beats.activity: Grab lunch in the school cafeteria with colleagues.
beats.reasoning: Rainy day keeps her indoors; she's gregarious and chatty, so eating in the cafeteria with coworkers fits her personality.

beats.activity: Attend department meeting in the staff lounge.
beats.reasoning: Department meetings are part of her regular schedule; the rain means no early dismissal or outdoor activities.

beats.activity: Drive back to Seattle via I-5, rain clearing by mid-afternoon.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure has ended by this time; the main I-5 route is reliable; she heads home to change before evening plans.

beats.activity: Stop by Whole Foods to pick up salmon, vegetables, and ingredients for meal prep.
beats.reasoning: She meal-prepped yesterday but needs fresh ingredients; this is her routine errand, keeping her organized spreadsheet of expenses in mind.

beats.activity: Meal prep at home: cook salmon, roast vegetables, portion rice into containers.
beats.reasoning: She's meticulous about meal prep and planning; this is a regular Tuesday evening task that keeps her organized for the week.

beats.activity: Walk to Swallow's Nest Fly Shop to browse new inventory and chat with staff.
beats.location_name: Swallow's Nest Fly Shop (Capitol Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Rain has mostly cleared by late afternoon; she's passionate about fly fishing and enjoys this local neighborhood spot; gregarious nature means she'll chat with staff.

beats.activity: Meet a close friend for dinner at a cozy Capitol Hill restaurant.
beats.reasoning: She values time with close friends; a Tuesday evening dinner fits her social, gregarious personality and her low energy from yesterday (45/100) calls for good company.

beats.activity: Walk home through Capitol Hill and spend quiet time journaling, organizing spreadsheets, and winding down.
beats.reasoning: She's back home before dark; quiet evening routine reflects her workaholic nature balancing with self-care; meticulous expense tracking is part of her evening ritual.

diary:
Woke up to grey skies and rain drumming against the windows—typical Seattle Tuesday. Checked my phone while sipping coffee and saw the SR-520 closure alert, so I made sure to head south on I-5 instead. The drive wasn't too bad, actually.

Teaching felt good today. Had the juniors doing titrations in lab, and watching them nail the color-change endpoint never gets old. Grabbed lunch with a couple colleagues in the cafeteria—we complained about the department meeting that followed, but it was actually fine.

By the time I drove back, the rain had mostly cleared. Stopped at Whole Foods on the way home and got ingredients for meal prep. Something about portioning salmon and roasting vegetables on a quiet evening just settles me. Later, I wandered over to Swallow's Nest to check out the new fly patterns—the staff there know me by now, which is nice.

Ended the night with dinner at Altura with a close friend, then a long walk home through the neighborhood. Journaled a bit before bed. Felt grounded.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure had me planning my route early—I didn't want stress before the day even started. Teaching kept me engaged; those lab moments remind me why I do this job. The department meeting could've been draining, but I went in with low expectations and that helped. I chose the Whole Foods stop deliberately—meal prep is my way of taking control when the world feels chaotic. Browsing the fly shop was pure leisure, unrushed. Meeting my friend felt important after a full day of managing other people's learning. I needed that decompression time. By evening, my mood had shifted from the grey morning drowsiness into something more reflective and peaceful. The walk home through familiar streets sealed it.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
